topic: GPT-6 vs Claude Opus 4.7 vs Gemini 3.1: Developer Benchmark Comparison 2026

key_points: Detailed comparison of GPT-5.5 vs Claude Opus 4.7 vs Gemini 3.1 Pro for developers and builders
GPT-5.5 leads on Terminal-Bench 2.0 (82.7%), Claude Opus 4.7 leads on SWE-bench Pro (64.3%)
Gemini 3.1 Pro is the most affordable frontier model at $2/M input, $12/M output
Each model has a distinct domain strength making single-model choice suboptimal
Head-to-head Claude Opus 4.7 vs Gemini 3.1 Pro with real developer testing
Gemini 3.1 leads on multimodal tasks (82.8 vs 64.3) and GPQA Diamond (94.3%)
Claude Opus 4.7 leads on coding (87.6% SWE-bench Verified vs 80.6%) and agentic tool use (77.3% MCP-Atlas)
Context window: both support 1M tokens; Claude has 128K max output vs Gemini 65K
Frontier model showdown with code examples and real-world task testing
Covers pricing, context windows, API features, and developer workflow integration
GPT-5.5 preferred for terminal/CLI workflows; Claude for multi-file code agents
Notes that MMLU is now saturated — recommends SWE-bench, GPQA, ARC-AGI-2 as meaningful benchmarks
Decision framework for choosing between Gemini 3.1 Pro, Claude Opus 4.7, and GPT-5.5
Gemini 3.1 Pro batch API at 50% off brings input to $1/M — ideal for async, high-volume workloads
Claude Opus 4.7 task budgets feature enables cost-controlled agentic loops
GPT-5.5 72% token-efficient vs Claude Opus 4.7 on equivalent coding tasks
GPT-6 pre-training completed March 24, 2026 at Stargate (Abilene, TX) using 100K+ H100/B200 GPUs
Expected model codenamed 'Spud' shipped as GPT-5.5, not GPT-6 — Q3-Q4 2026 for actual GPT-6
Polymarket odds: ~45% by June 30, ~84% by Dec 31, 2026
GPT-6 expected to deliver 40%+ performance gain over GPT-5.5 across coding, reasoning, agents

stats.fact: Claude Opus 4.7 scores 87.6% on SWE-bench Verified, the industry benchmark for real-world software engineering tasks
Claude Opus 4.7 leads SWE-bench Pro at 64.3%, beating GPT-5.5 (58.6%) and DeepSeek V4 Pro (55.4%) on complex multi-file GitHub issues
Gemini 3.1 Pro achieves 94.3% on GPQA Diamond — the highest score ever recorded on this PhD-level science benchmark, surpassing GPT-5.4 (92.8%)
Gemini 3.1 Pro scores 77.1% on ARC-AGI-2 — more than double Gemini 3 Pro's score
GPT-5.5 scores 88.7% on SWE-bench Verified and 82.7% on Terminal-Bench 2.0, leading on CLI and shell workflow tasks
GPT-5.5 delivers 72% fewer output tokens than Claude Opus 4.7 on equivalent coding tasks, reducing operational costs
Gemini 3.1 Pro costs $2/M input, $12/M output — 2.5x cheaper on input and 50% cheaper on output vs Claude Opus 4.7 ($5/$25)
GPT-5.5 Standard and Claude Opus 4.7 share the same input price ($5/M) but GPT-5.5 output is 20% more expensive ($30 vs $25/M)
GPT-6 pre-training completed March 24, 2026 at Stargate data center; 84% Polymarket odds for release by December 31, 2026
GPT-6 expected to deliver 40%+ performance gain over current models in coding, reasoning, and agentic tasks
Claude Opus 4.7 MCP-Atlas scaled tool use: 77.3% vs Gemini 3.1 Pro (73.9%), indicating stronger multi-tool agentic performance
Gemini 3.1 Pro multimodal score: 82.8 vs Claude Opus 4.7 (64.3) — the largest performance gap between the two models

angles: GPT-6 is not released yet (May 2026) — this comparison covers the current frontier landscape while previewing what GPT-6 changes, making it evergreen for developers choosing today vs. waiting
The 'GPT-6 vs. Claude vs. Gemini' framing captures massive search volume from developers wanting to know if they should switch models or wait for GPT-6
No single model wins every benchmark — the real developer insight is which model wins your specific workflow: Claude for agentic coding, Gemini for science/multimodal, GPT-5.5 for CLI/terminal
Token economics angle: GPT-5.5 is 72% more token-efficient than Claude Opus 4.7 on coding — a $100K API budget goes dramatically further depending on your model choice
The upcoming GPT-6 launch creates urgency: developers need to understand the current state before GPT-6 reorders the rankings in Q3/Q4 2026
Gemini 3.1 Pro's 1M context at $2/M input makes it 2.5x cheaper than competitors — for cost-sensitive teams, this is the main differentiator
Claude Opus 4.7's new features (task budgets, file-system memory, server-side compaction) are specifically designed for long-running agent loops that developers are building in 2026
